source: synthetic contract clause shaped after common credit-agreement language
text: The borrower shall deliver audited annual financial statements to the lender within 90 days after the end of each fiscal year, unless the lender waives the requirement in writing.
context: Obligation language creates a future duty, not proof that delivery occurred.
Waiver language is an exception or condition, not proof that the exception was exercised.

text: No contractor may access production systems unless sponsored by a manager. Sponsorship expires 90 days after approval unless renewed.
context: Policy rules constrain future access decisions.
Expired sponsorship should not automatically prove unauthorized access without an access event.

text: Anyone approving a reimbursement over $10,000 must not be the requester, and a manager of the requester also counts as conflicted.
context: Conflict rules define prohibited approval relationships.
A rule should not be written as a current violation until an approval event is supplied.

text: The March audit report states that invoice R-42 was submitted by Dana and approved by Ilya, but the report does not say whether Ilya manages Dana.
context: A source report can support submitted-by and approved-by facts.
Absence of a management statement is not proof of no management relationship.

text: If the borrower defaults, the lender may accelerate repayment and terminate the revolving commitment.
context: Default is a condition for a right; this sentence does not say default occurred.
May language creates permission or entitlement, not proof that the right was exercised.

text: Verbal amendments are valid only if witnessed by two non-beneficiaries simultaneously.
context: Do not treat a described amendment as valid unless the witness condition is satisfied.

text: Correction: the termination notice was sent on April 10, not April 1, and the old date came from a draft schedule.
context: Explicit correction language can justify a retraction/replacement plan when an old fact exists.
Draft schedules may have lower source priority than correction notes.

text: Premium customers receive priority support except customers with unpaid invoices; gold partners override unpaid-invoice downgrades unless fraud is suspected.
context: Exception and override ladders should preserve rule structure.
Do not turn priority support or downgrade into current facts without a specific customer state and query.
